questions.question: Who is eligible to connect Microsoft Teams to Notion AI?

options.text: Any member of the Microsoft Teams workspace
options.explanation: Regular members cannot grant the required application permissions; only admins with the proper role can.

options.text: A Microsoft Teams admin with a Privileged Role Administrator or Global Administrator role, who is also the Notion workspace owner using the same email address
options.explanation: Both conditions are required: the admin role in Teams and ownership of the Notion workspace, and the email addresses must match.

options.text: Only the Notion workspace owner, regardless of Teams role
options.explanation: The Notion owner also needs the matching Teams admin role; ownership alone is insufficient.

options.text: Guest users in the Notion workspace
options.explanation: Guest users are explicitly excluded from using the AI connector.

questions.question: How long can the initial Microsoft Teams sync take?

options.text: Up to 24 hours
options.explanation: The documentation states the initial sync may take longer than 24 hours.

options.text: Up to 48 hours
options.explanation: While 48 hours is possible, the maximum mentioned is 72 hours.

options.text: Up to 72 hours
options.explanation: The article specifies the initial sync may take up to 72 hours depending on data volume.

options.text: Immediately after connection
options.explanation: Syncing requires time to process channels, messages, and files; it is not instantaneous.

questions.question: How far back can the connector access Microsoft Teams messages?

options.text: Six months from the setup date
options.explanation: The limit is longer than six months.

options.text: One year from the setup date
options.explanation: The integration can read messages dating back one year from when it is set up.

options.text: Two years from the setup date
options.explanation: Only a one‑year window is mentioned.

options.text: There is no time limit; all history is available
options.explanation: A one‑year limit is explicitly defined.

questions.question: What is the default set of Microsoft Teams channels that Notion AI syncs?

options.text: Only public channels
options.explanation: Private and shared channels are also included by default.

options.text: Only private 1:1 chats
options.explanation: The default sync is broader than just 1:1 chats.

options.text: All private (1:1 and group chats), public, and shared channels in the selected teams
options.explanation: The article states this exact default behavior.

options.text: Only shared channels
options.explanation: Shared channels are part of the default set, but not the only ones.

questions.question: If a user is added to a private Multi‑DM after the connector is set up, which messages can they query through Notion AI?

options.text: All historic messages from the beginning of the chat
options.explanation: Permission checks limit access to messages that existed before the user joined.

options.text: Only messages posted after they were added to the chat
options.explanation: The integration respects Teams permissions: a user can only ask about messages that existed while they were a member.

options.text: No messages at all until the next sync
options.explanation: They can query new messages; the limitation is about historic content.

options.text: Only messages that were edited after they joined
options.explanation: Editing status does not change the basic access rule.

questions.question: How often does Notion AI monitor Microsoft Teams for edited or deleted messages?

options.text: Every 5 minutes
options.explanation: The article specifies a longer interval.

options.text: Every hour
options.explanation: Edited or deleted content is checked on an hourly basis.

options.text: Once per day
options.explanation: Checks are more frequent than daily.

options.text: In real‑time as changes occur
options.explanation: The system does not provide real‑time monitoring.

questions.question: Which statement about external messages and channels is correct?

options.text: External messages are indexed and searchable in Notion AI
options.explanation: External content is explicitly excluded.

options.text: Only external channels are indexed, while internal ones are ignored
options.explanation: The opposite is true; internal content is indexed.

options.text: All external messages and channels are excluded from indexing
options.explanation: The article states that external messages/channels are not indexed.

options.text: Only messages from external users are excluded, but external channels are indexed
options.explanation: Both external users and channels are excluded.

questions.question: How can a user limit a search to only Microsoft Teams content within Notion AI?

options.text: By typing a special command in the Teams chat
options.explanation: The toggle is performed in Notion, not in Teams.

options.text: In the Notion AI entry point, click “Find in” and select Microsoft Teams
options.explanation: The UI provides a “Find in” toggle to choose the source.

options.text: Changing the workspace settings to restrict sources
options.explanation: The setting is per‑search, not a workspace‑wide configuration.

options.text: It is not possible to limit the scope; all sources are always searched
options.explanation: The product explicitly allows toggling between sources.

questions.question: What happens to Microsoft Teams content in Notion AI when a team is removed from a shared channel?

options.text: All messages become searchable instantly for remaining members
options.explanation: Removal revokes access to new messages; older messages have a limited window.

options.text: New messages are no longer searchable, but older messages remain searchable for up to one hour
options.explanation: The article specifies that after removal, newer messages are inaccessible, while older ones stay searchable for about an hour.

options.text: Both new and old messages remain searchable forever for all members
options.explanation: Access to new messages is cut off, and old messages eventually expire.

options.text: All content from that shared channel is immediately deleted from Notion AI
options.explanation: Only searchability changes; data is not instantly deleted.

questions.question: After disconnecting Microsoft Teams from Notion, when is the synced data deleted?

options.text: Immediately upon disconnection
options.explanation: The article notes a short delay before deletion.

options.text: Within 24 hours of the disconnection request
options.explanation: Data is removed from searchability instantly, but actual deletion occurs within one day.

options.text: After seven days
options.explanation: The retention period is shorter than a week.

options.text: The data is never deleted and remains archived
options.explanation: The connector explicitly deletes the data after disconnection.
